title: space_set two-phase validate-then-apply is correct and should not be simplified
description: The two-phase structure (Phase 1: validate all member patches before any write; Phase 2: apply) is the correct implementation of RFC 8620 §7.1 atomicity for input errors. A contributor might suggest merging validation into the apply loop to reduce code. That would break atomicity: a combined {name: Foo, members/x: {role: bad}} patch would write the name before discovering the role error, leaving partial state. The two-pass structure must be preserved. The documented Phase 2 server-error atomicity gap (name written before a later store failure) is a real known limitation but requires a DB transaction-level fix, not elimination of the two-phase pattern.
close_reason: Decision recorded; no code change needed
